short_summary: Iran deal may ease energy costs
rationale: Portugal is primarily affected indirectly through potential energy price relief from a US-Iran deal; no direct regional security or economic shocks this cycle.

top_events.title: Iran signals Hormuz reopening tied to peace deal
top_events.summary: A US-Iran deal could lower global oil and gas prices, offering indirect relief to Portuguese consumers and businesses still facing elevated energy costs.

top_events.title: UAE to unlock frozen Iranian funds amid US ceasefire push
top_events.summary: Growing momentum toward a US-Iran deal adds to the prospect of eased global energy markets, with modest indirect benefit for Portugal.

top_events.title: EU accession process launched for Ukraine and Moldova
top_events.summary: Long-term EU budget and structural fund implications for Portugal as enlargement progresses, though effects are distant and gradual.
